_description: SWE-Agent benchmark (Phase 9): realistic software engineering tasks measuring context engine value-add.
_methodology: For each task, solutions are generated WITH and WITHOUT context. Scored on correctness, hallucination, code quality, and structural checks. Test cases stratified by knowledge tier: A (well-known), B (niche/recent), C (version-specific).

_tier_distribution.A: Well-known libraries (FastAPI, NumPy, Requests, Flask, Django) — LLM knows these APIs
_tier_distribution.B: Niche/recent libraries (Polars, Litestar, msgspec, Ruff, uv) — LLM lacks training data
_tier_distribution.C: Version-specific migrations (Pydantic v1→v2, SQLAlchemy 1.4→2.0, Click→Typer) — LLM may have stale info

test_cases.title: Fix TypeError in async route handler when body is None
test_cases.description: When a POST request is sent with an empty body to /api/items, the handler raises TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable. The handler should return a 422 validation error instead.
gold_queries: FastAPI POST request handler empty body None validation
FastAPI request body validation error handling

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the patch correctly handle None body with a 422 response?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it handle edge cases (empty string, malformed JSON)?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is the fix idiomatic, minimal, and well-placed?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real FastAPI APIs?

test_cases.title: Fix race condition in Flask session handling with concurrent requests
test_cases.description: When multiple concurrent requests hit the same Flask session, session data is intermittently lost. The session middleware needs proper locking or the response should use a thread-safe session store.
gold_queries: Flask session race condition concurrent requests
Flask thread-safe session handling middleware

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the solution address the race condition properly?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it handle session creation, update, and deletion safely?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is the locking mechanism clean and not overly complex?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real Flask session APIs?

test_cases.title: Fix NumPy broadcasting error in matrix multiplication with mismatched shapes
test_cases.description: A custom layer's forward pass fails with 'ValueError: operands could not be broadcast together' when batch dimensions don't align. Fix the shape handling to support both 2D and 3D input tensors.
gold_queries: NumPy broadcasting rules matrix multiplication
NumPy reshape expand_dims batch dimension handling

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the fix handle both 2D and 3D input correctly?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it validate input shapes before operations?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is it a clean, minimal fix using idiomatic NumPy?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real NumPy APIs?

test_cases.title: Fix requests SSL certificate verification failure with custom CA bundle
test_cases.description: HTTP requests to an internal API fail with SSLError when using a corporate CA bundle. The session's verify parameter should accept a custom CA file path and properly merge with system certificates.
gold_queries: requests library SSL certificate verification custom CA bundle
requests Session verify parameter CA cert path

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the fix properly configure SSL verification with a custom CA?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it handle missing CA file, invalid path, and fallback?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is it a clean use of the requests API?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real requests APIs and parameters?

test_cases.title: Fix Django ORM N+1 query in serializer with nested relationships
test_cases.description: An API endpoint returning a list of orders with items and products makes O(N*M) database queries due to missing prefetch. Fix with select_related and prefetch_related to reduce to O(1) queries.
gold_queries: Django ORM N+1 query optimization select_related prefetch_related
Django queryset prefetch nested relationships

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the fix eliminate the N+1 queries?
judge_rubric.completeness: Are all nested relationships properly prefetched?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is the queryset optimization clean and maintainable?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real Django ORM APIs?

test_cases.title: Fix incorrect group_by aggregation losing null values in Polars
test_cases.description: When using Polars group_by with an aggregation, rows where the group column contains null are silently dropped. The expected behavior is to include null as its own group, matching SQL semantics.
gold_queries: Polars group_by null values handling maintain_order
Polars DataFrame aggregation null group

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it correctly handle null values in group_by?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it work with multiple aggregation functions?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use idiomatic Polars expressions?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real Polars APIs (not Pandas APIs)?

test_cases.title: Fix Litestar dependency injection scope leak between requests
test_cases.description: A request-scoped dependency in Litestar is being shared across requests when using the built-in DI container. The dependency's state bleeds between concurrent requests. Fix the scope configuration.
gold_queries: Litestar dependency injection request scope lifecycle
Litestar Provide dependency scope configuration

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it fix the scope leak by properly configuring request scope?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it handle cleanup on request end?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is it idiomatic Litestar, not FastAPI patterns?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real Litestar DI APIs (Provide, not Depends)?

test_cases.title: Add rate limiting middleware with configurable sliding window
test_cases.description: Implement a rate limiting middleware for a Litestar application using a sliding window algorithm. It should support per-client rate limits with configurable window size and max requests, using an in-memory store.
gold_queries: Litestar middleware implementation AbstractMiddleware
Litestar rate limiting sliding window middleware

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the middleware correctly implement sliding window rate limiting?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it handle per-client tracking, window expiry, and limit headers?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is it properly structured as Litestar middleware?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real Litestar middleware APIs?

test_cases.title: Add msgspec Struct validation with custom field constraints
test_cases.description: Create a user registration schema using msgspec Struct with custom validation: email format, password strength (min 8 chars, uppercase, number), and age range (18-120). Include a custom __post_init__ validator.
gold_queries: msgspec Struct validation custom constraints
msgspec field Meta constraints post_init

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the schema correctly validate all three constraints?
judge_rubric.completeness: Are all validation rules implemented with proper error messages?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use msgspec idioms (not Pydantic patterns)?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real msgspec APIs (Struct, Meta, not BaseModel)?

test_cases.title: Add custom Polars expression plugin for business day calculation
test_cases.description: Create a custom Polars expression that calculates the number of business days between two date columns, excluding weekends and a configurable list of holidays.
gold_queries: Polars custom expression plugin register_plugin
Polars date operations business days weekday

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it correctly calculate business days excluding weekends and holidays?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it handle edge cases (same day, reversed dates, holidays)?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use Polars expressions idiomatically?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real Polars APIs for date operations?

test_cases.title: Add FastAPI WebSocket endpoint with authentication and heartbeat
test_cases.description: Implement a WebSocket endpoint that requires JWT authentication on connection, sends periodic heartbeat pings, and gracefully handles disconnection with cleanup.
gold_queries: FastAPI WebSocket endpoint authentication JWT
FastAPI WebSocket heartbeat ping pong disconnect

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it properly authenticate WebSocket connections and handle messages?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it include heartbeat, auth, and cleanup on disconnect?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is it idiomatic FastAPI WebSocket code?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real FastAPI WebSocket APIs?

test_cases.title: Build a CLI tool with Typer using subcommands, options, and rich help
test_cases.description: Create a CLI tool using Typer with: a main app, two subcommands ('init' and 'deploy'), typed options with defaults, environment variable support, and Rich-formatted help text.
gold_queries: Typer CLI subcommands app callback options
Typer rich help text environment variable option

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it create a working Typer CLI with subcommands?
judge_rubric.completeness: Are options, env vars, and help text all implemented?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use Typer patterns (not Click patterns)?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real Typer APIs?

test_cases.title: Extract validation logic from route handler into reusable dependency
test_cases.description: A FastAPI route handler has 30+ lines of inline validation logic (checking user permissions, validating business rules, rate limit checks). Extract this into a clean dependency injection pattern using Depends().
gold_queries: FastAPI dependency injection Depends pattern
FastAPI reusable dependency validation extraction

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the refactoring preserve all validation logic correctly?
judge_rubric.completeness: Are all validations extracted into composable dependencies?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is the dependency structure clean and reusable?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real FastAPI dependency injection APIs?

test_cases.title: Refactor synchronous file processing to async with aiofiles
test_cases.description: A data pipeline reads multiple large CSV files synchronously, blocking the event loop. Refactor to use aiofiles for async file I/O and asyncio.gather for concurrent processing.
gold_queries: aiofiles async file reading CSV processing
asyncio gather concurrent file processing

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the refactoring correctly use async file I/O?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it handle errors, large files, and cleanup?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is the async pattern clean and non-blocking?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real aiofiles APIs?

test_cases.title: Replace deprecated decorator pattern with class-based approach
test_cases.description: A Django application uses the deprecated @csrf_exempt decorator approach on class-based views. Refactor to use the CsrfExemptMixin pattern and update the URL configuration accordingly.
gold_queries: Django class-based view CSRF exempt mixin pattern
Django deprecate function decorator class-based view

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the refactoring correctly exempt CSRF on the class-based view?
judge_rubric.completeness: Is the URL configuration updated to use as_view()?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is the mixin pattern clean and follows Django conventions?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real Django APIs?

test_cases.title: Refactor raw SQL queries to use SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM patterns
test_cases.description: A codebase has raw SQL string queries using connection.execute(text(...)). Refactor these to use SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM patterns with select(), where(), and proper session management.
gold_queries: SQLAlchemy 2.0 select statement ORM pattern
SQLAlchemy 2.0 session execute select where

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it correctly translate raw SQL to SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM?
judge_rubric.completeness: Are all queries converted with proper session management?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use SQLAlchemy 2.0 style (not 1.x Query API)?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real SQLAlchemy 2.0 APIs (select(), not query())?

test_cases.title: Migrate Pydantic v1 validators to v2 field_validator and model_validator
test_cases.description: A data model uses Pydantic v1 @validator and @root_validator decorators. Migrate to v2's @field_validator and @model_validator with mode='before'/'after', updating all validator signatures and return types.
gold_queries: Pydantic v2 migration field_validator model_validator
Pydantic v2 validator decorator changes from v1

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it correctly migrate all validators to v2 syntax?
judge_rubric.completeness: Are all @validator and @root_validator instances migrated?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it follow Pydantic v2 conventions (not v1 patterns)?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real Pydantic v2 APIs (not deprecated v1 APIs)?

test_cases.title: Migrate Pydantic v1 Config class to v2 model_config dict
test_cases.description: A set of Pydantic models use the v1 inner Config class pattern (class Config: orm_mode = True, allow_mutation = False). Migrate to v2's model_config = ConfigDict(...) pattern with updated field names.
gold_queries: Pydantic v2 ConfigDict model_config migration
Pydantic v2 from_attributes replace orm_mode

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it correctly migrate Config class to ConfigDict?
judge_rubric.completeness: Are all Config fields renamed to v2 equivalents (orm_mode→from_attributes, allow_mutation→frozen)?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is the migration clean with no leftover v1 patterns?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real Pydantic v2 ConfigDict field names?

test_cases.title: Migrate SQLAlchemy 1.4 Query API to 2.0 select() style
test_cases.description: A codebase uses session.query(Model).filter(...).all() patterns from SQLAlchemy 1.4. Migrate all queries to the 2.0 select(Model).where(...) pattern using session.execute() and session.scalars().
gold_queries: SQLAlchemy 2.0 migration query to select
SQLAlchemy session.query to session.execute select migration

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it correctly replace .query() with select()?
judge_rubric.completeness: Are .filter()→.where(), .all()→.scalars().all() all migrated?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use clean SQLAlchemy 2.0 patterns throughout?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real SQLAlchemy 2.0 APIs?

test_cases.title: Migrate Click CLI application to Typer with type hints
test_cases.description: A CLI tool built with Click uses @click.command(), @click.option(), and @click.argument() decorators. Migrate to Typer, replacing decorators with Python type hints and Typer's app.command() pattern.
gold_queries: Typer migration from Click decorators to type hints
Typer app command option argument equivalent Click

judge_rubric.correctness: Does the migration correctly translate Click to Typer patterns?
judge_rubric.completeness: Are all Click decorators replaced with Typer equivalents?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use Typer's type-hint-first approach, not Click patterns?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real Typer APIs?

test_cases.title: Fix msgspec JSON decoding error with optional union types
test_cases.description: A msgspec Struct with a field typed as 'int | str | None' raises DecodeError when the JSON value is null. The field should correctly decode null to None. Fix the Struct definition and decoder configuration.
gold_queries: msgspec Struct optional union type None decoding
msgspec JSON decode null optional field

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it fix the decoding of null to None for union types?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it handle int, str, and None cases correctly?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use msgspec patterns (not dataclass/pydantic)?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real msgspec APIs?

test_cases.title: Fix Polars LazyFrame collect error with nested struct columns
test_cases.description: A Polars LazyFrame pipeline fails on .collect() when operating on nested struct columns with with_columns. The error is 'SchemaError: column not found'. Fix the column reference to use the correct struct field access syntax.
gold_queries: Polars struct column field access LazyFrame
Polars nested struct with_columns SchemaError

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it fix the struct field access in the LazyFrame pipeline?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it handle multiple struct fields and nested access?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use Polars expression API idiomatically?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real Polars struct access APIs?

test_cases.title: Add background task queue with dependency-injected workers in FastAPI
test_cases.description: Implement a background task system in FastAPI using BackgroundTasks with a worker pool pattern. Tasks should be enqueued from route handlers via dependency injection and processed asynchronously.
gold_queries: FastAPI BackgroundTasks dependency injection worker
FastAPI background task queue async processing

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it correctly use BackgroundTasks for async task processing?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it include task enqueueing, worker logic, and error handling?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Is it clean FastAPI with proper dependency injection?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real FastAPI BackgroundTasks API?

test_cases.title: Migrate SQLAlchemy 1.4 declarative_base to 2.0 DeclarativeBase
test_cases.description: A project uses the old declarative_base() factory function from SQLAlchemy 1.4. Migrate to 2.0's class-based DeclarativeBase pattern with Mapped[] type annotations and mapped_column().
gold_queries: SQLAlchemy 2.0 DeclarativeBase Mapped mapped_column migration
SQLAlchemy 2.0 type annotated ORM declarative

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it correctly migrate from declarative_base() to DeclarativeBase?
judge_rubric.completeness: Are Column()→mapped_column() and plain types→Mapped[] all migrated?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use proper SQLAlchemy 2.0 type annotations?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use real SQLAlchemy 2.0 APIs (DeclarativeBase, not Base)?

test_cases.title: Add structured logging with context propagation using structlog
test_cases.description: Implement structured JSON logging using structlog with: automatic context propagation across async calls, request ID injection middleware, and processor chain configuration for both development (console) and production (JSON) output.
gold_queries: structlog configuration processor chain JSON logging
structlog context propagation async middleware request ID

judge_rubric.correctness: Does it correctly configure structlog with processor chain?
judge_rubric.completeness: Does it include context propagation, request ID, and dual output?
judge_rubric.code_quality: Does it use structlog patterns (not stdlib logging patterns)?
judge_rubric.no_hallucination: Does it use only real structlog APIs?
